mission: Identify whether the final unresolved Paper 35 integer n_punct is a genuinely new frontier or simply the old full puncture-resolved horizon-algebra gap in exact disguise.

theorem_1_where_n_punct_lives.statement: The integer n_punct is not a reduced gauge-center datum. It is a puncture-resolved topological datum of the horizon line-bundle sector seen by the fermionic bridge. So it necessarily lives in the noncentral puncture-resolved horizon algebra, not in the already-closed reduced bridge class.

theorem_2_paper19_equivalence.statement: Deriving n_punct on the current IO program is equivalent in strength to deriving the full puncture-resolved horizon algebra plus the required Abelianization/spin^c lift. That is precisely the long-standing unresolved Paper 19 reduction/extension gap, not a new standalone Paper 35 theorem debt.

final_boundary.safe_statement: No further theorem-grade Paper 35 progress remains on the current stack. The remaining integer n_punct is exactly the old full puncture-resolved horizon-algebra/Abelianization gap in Paper 19 form. Paper 35 is therefore closed as far as the present stack permits.
